title: Number Systems, Signed Quantities, and Reliable Operations · Worked Case: Operation Order
summary: Reconcile inventory gains, losses, temperatures, elevations, and account changes from mixed records. Planned artifact: Add and verify the math beg operation order increment in a signed-quantity inventory reconciliation. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Evaluate grouped expressions with a documented order of operations and intermediate checks.
Model direction and change with signed numbers and explain each operation on a number line.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.python:
"""applied-mathematics-beginner cumulative model portfolio."""

from math import exp, log, sqrt


def reasonableness(value: float, lower: float, upper: float) -> bool:
    return lower <= value <= upper

steps.title: Calculate Operation Order evidence
steps.instruction: A measured quantity starts at 22 units, changes by −9 units, then by +8 units. Calculate the final quantity. Use the result to decide Operation Order for a signed-quantity inventory reconciliation.
steps.why: The Operation Order result for a signed-quantity inventory reconciliation is incomplete until its unit, assumptions, scale, and tolerance survive a reasonableness check.

content.text: Preserve the signs and compute 22 − 9 + 8; compare with the starting scale.
